Package i5.cb.graph.diagram

Class DiagramEdge

  • java.lang.Object
    • java.awt.Component
      • java.awt.Container
        • javax.swing.JComponent
          • i5.cb.graph.diagram.DiagramEdge
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    DiagramObject, i5.cb.graph.zooming.Zoomable, java.beans.PropertyChangeListener, java.util.EventListener

    public class DiagramEdge
extends javax.swing.JComponent
implements DiagramObject, java.beans.PropertyChangeListener, i5.cb.graph.zooming.Zoomable
    This class represents the binary relations or edges between two userObjects. It draws an edge between two DiagramNodes and makes sure that the line lies somehow near the DiagramNode instance that represents the this edge's userobject. It also moves this DiagramNode if the source- or the destinationnode is moved Created: Tue Dec 18 10:14:17 2001
    See Also:
    Serialized Form

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor Description
      DiagramEdge​(DiagramNode nodeOnEdge, DiagramObject source, DiagramObject destination, DiagramClass diagramClass)
      Creates a new diagramEdge with the provided diagramNode The diagramNode's location is not changed
      DiagramEdge​(java.lang.Object userObject, DiagramObject source, DiagramObject destination, DiagramClass diagramClass)
      Creates a diagramEdge with a new diagramNode using a provided userobject the diagramNode's location is NOT set by this diagramClass.

      Gets the DiagramDesktop this DiagramObject is currently shown in.

      Returns a vector containing the edges this edge is source or destination of.

      Gets a node's (must be source- or destinationnode of this edge) peer.

      Returns a shape containing both this edge's shape and the shape of the node sitting in this edge
      DiagramObject getSource​()

      Returns the userobject this DiagramObject is associated with.
      boolean hasFixedPosition​()
      Tells whether this edge is moveable or not
      boolean isComponentVisible​()
      Tells whether this edge's component is visible or not.The result is similar to the result of this.getnode().isComponentVisible()
      boolean isReflexive​()  
      boolean isShown​()  
      boolean isSmallComponentVisible​()
      Tells whether this edge's smallComponent is visible or not
      void paint​(java.awt.Graphics g1)
      Every object implementing this inferface should have a paint method as we are dealing with a graphical representation of objects and their relations.
      void propertyChange​(java.beans.PropertyChangeEvent evt)  
      void redrawEdge​()
      Make sure that this edge is redrawn.
      boolean removeEdge​(DiagramEdge e)
      Removes a diagramEdge by calling the removeEdge-method of the diagramNode sitting on this Edge
      boolean setComponentVisible​()
      Makes this edge's component (which is actually the Component of this edge's node) visible on the desktop The result is similar to the result of this.getnode().setComponentVisible

      • updateEdgeStroke

        public void updateEdgeStroke​()
        Reload EdgeStroke and Edge color from DiagramClass

      • getArrowWidth

        public int getArrowWidth​()
        Computes the width of a the arrow head(s) of this edge. The width determines how much space we need to paint the edge stroke including its heads.

        public void straightenEdge​()
        Set the edge middle point to the arithmetic center between the source abd destination of this edge

      • addEdge

        public final void addEdge​(DiagramEdge edge)
        Adds a new DiagramEdge to the node sitting on this edge. This is called by another diagramEdge instance if this edge is the source or destination of this other edge
        Specified by:
        addEdge in interface DiagramObject
        Parameters:
        edge - the edge we are source or destination of.

      • removeEdge

        public boolean removeEdge​(DiagramEdge e)
        Removes a diagramEdge by calling the removeEdge-method of the diagramNode sitting on this Edge
        Specified by:
        removeEdge in interface DiagramObject
        Parameters:
        e - the egde to be removed
        Returns:
        the result of DiagramNode.removeEdge(e)

      • getEdges

        public java.util.Vector getEdges​()
        Returns a vector containing the edges this edge is source or destination of. The result is similar to the result of this.getnode().getEdges()
        Specified by:
        getEdges in interface DiagramObject
        Returns:
        a Vector value containing the edges this edge is source or destination of.

      • erase

        public void erase​()
        Removes this DiagramEdge from the diagramDesktop. The Nodes that are connected by this edge are not removed.
        Specified by:
        erase in interface DiagramObject

      • isComponentVisible

        public boolean isComponentVisible​()
        Tells whether this edge's component is visible or not.The result is similar to the result of this.getnode().isComponentVisible()
        Specified by:
        isComponentVisible in interface DiagramObject
        Returns:
        a boolean value

      • setComponentVisible

        public boolean setComponentVisible​()
        Makes this edge's component (which is actually the Component of this edge's node) visible on the desktop The result is similar to the result of this.getnode().setComponentVisible
        Specified by:
        setComponentVisible in interface DiagramObject
        Returns:
        false iff the component is null

        Specified by:
        setSmallComponentVisible in interface DiagramObject
        Returns:
        false iff the smallComponent is null

      • paint

        public void paint​(java.awt.Graphics g1)
        Description copied from interface: DiagramObject
        Every object implementing this inferface should have a paint method as we are dealing with a graphical representation of objects and their relations. Using DiagramObjects without somehow painting them doesn't seem to make sense.
        Specified by:
        paint in interface DiagramObject
        Overrides:
        paint in class javax.swing.JComponent
        Parameters:
        g1 - a Graphics value

      • getPeer

        public DiagramObject getPeer​(DiagramObject diagObj)
        Gets a node's (must be source- or destinationnode of this edge) peer.
        Parameters:
        diagObj - the source- or destination-diagramObject of this edge.
        Returns:
        the edges sourcediagObject node if 'diagObj' is the edge's destination and the destination if 'diagObj' is the source

      • setOldFactor

        public void setOldFactor​(float oldFactor)
        Parameters:
        oldFactor - The oldFactor to set.

      • validateEdge

        public void validateEdge​()
        Correct the locations of end points after a move. Edges of edges can be misplaced because the graph editor failes to update them while moving a node. So we correct the misplacement when the mouse button is released (DiagramNodeMouseListener.java)

      • redrawEdge

        public void redrawEdge​()
        Make sure that this edge is redrawn.
